name: Agent memory market landscape
context_primitives: The market splits into (1) **model-host session tricks**—compaction, trimming, prompt caching; (2) **platform conversation state**—OpenAI Conversations/Sessions, Anthropic compaction APIs; (3) **application memory services**—Mem0, Zep, Letta, vector DB + RAG pipelines; (4) **IDE/agent hosts**—rules files, MCP resources, subagents. Buyers mix 1+2 for convenience and add 3 when personalization or cross-session facts must survive compaction.
deployment_pattern: Pick one primary store for durable facts (memory service or graph), one policy for transcript (summarize/trim), and tracing to verify recall quality.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: LongMemEval and LOCOMO-style long-dialog tasks are cited by vendors to compare memory layers vs. baselines; treat vendor numbers as marketing until reproduced.
ecosystem_integrations: MCP for attaching memory servers; LangGraph/LangChain for orchestration; observability vendors for drift and recall audits.
differentiators: Graph/temporal memory (Zep) vs. embedding store (classic RAG) vs. agent-editable blocks (Letta) vs. lightweight fact cache (Mem0).
significance_for_builders: Choose based on **temporal correctness** (do facts expire?), **editability** (does the agent rewrite memory?), and **latency budget** (p95 retrieval), not headline context-window size.
key_timeline_or_milestones: 2024–2026 wave of memory papers and products (Mem0 arXiv Apr 2025; Zep arXiv Jan 2025; Letta v1 blog Oct 2025).

primary_sources.title: RhinoInsight — control mechanisms for deep research context
